nodes.context: London gang led by the Kray twins, involved in murder, armed robbery, arson, protection rackets, gambling and assaults.
South London gang rival to the Krays' Firm, led by Charlie Richardson.
Governing body of organized crime in the United States, allied with the Kray twins in the 1960s.
Montreal-based Mafia family, satellite of the Bonanno crime family, sold stolen bonds to the Krays.
New York Mafia family, parent organization of the Cotroni crime family.

edges.context: The Richardson Gang was a rival of the Krays’ Firm.
The Kray twins formed an alliance with the American Mafia's Commission, working with Meyer Lansky and Angelo Bruno.
The Cotroni crime family sold stolen bearer bonds to the Krays, who redeemed them in London.
The Cotroni crime family was a Canadian satellite of the Bonanno crime family.
